title: hedyosmum narinoense
intro: Hedyosmum narinoense, also known as Narino's spurge or Narino's poke root, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Americas, characterized by its small, yellowish flowers and edible rhizomes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Hedyosmum narinoense include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction by boiling the rhizomes in water, or as a tincture by infusing the root in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
